post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 271

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I fdas am the moderator of Mafia Game 271 and lovematch13 is the co mod
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are a Vanilla Townie. You have no other power other than your vote.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Cop. Every night, you may target one person and determine their alignment.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Doctor. Every night you may heal someone, allowing them to survive if they would have otherwise died. You may not heal yourself two nights in a row.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Tracker. Every night you may target someone and learn who they visited.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Jailkeeper. Each night you may choose someone to put in jail. That person will be unable to perform any actions that night and night kills attempting to target that person will be blocked.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie. On the first time that you are attacked by the Mafia, that kill will be negated.

You win when all members of the Mafia are dead.
Quote:
You are a Mafia Goon. During the night you may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill.

You win when all members of the Town are dead.
Quote:
You are a Mafia Roleblocker. During the night you may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill. You may also target someone and that person will be unable to perform any actions that night. Your ability takes precedence over the jailkeeper.

You win when all members of the Town are dead.


Alive (3)
RAYRAY7512 ostriches88
awesomelion7 BluePhoenix88
Kea13


Dead(6)
++NDMath (Vanilla Townie)
Dream_123 Wesoar (Vanilla Townie)
AlienGirl05 (Vanilla Townie)
Socoobo (1 Shot Bulletproof)
Awesomeness_in_a_Bun (Mafia Goon)
+mathjay Madelyn (Tracker)


Events
Day 1: Wesoar hanged
Night 1: NDMath died
Day 2: AlienGirl05 hanged
Night 2: No one died
Day 3: No hang
Night 3: Socoobo died
Day 4: Awesomeness_in_a_Bun hanged
Night 4: Madelyn died


PM Prods
 


Warnings
Wesoar: OOT


Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins at 12:00:00 EDT 9 August 2023.

post_rendered:
mechanical notes that some variation of should get stated at some point so why not now

Do not claim your role except in certain circumstances. This includes if you're a vanilla townie (if the mafia know you're a vanilla townie, it's easier for them to figure out who the power roles are).

Those circumstances being
1) You are very likely to be hung otherwise. This one is only relevant towards the end of the day phase. Nobody is anywhere near being hung right now just since we haven't had much discussion yet.
2) We have collectively agreed to massclaim our roles. The purpose of this is a last effort to get information out there and solve the game. In newbie games, this usually happens on day 3.
3) You have critical information, for example if you are a cop and you received a guilty result.

Kea13 wrote:
Dream_123 wrote:
5. No and I’m not going to say something confusing in a newbie game like I might elsewhere

ok
lets talk about this

ur absolutely right
a townie shouldnt blatantly admit theyre mafia

but u cant just stop here and conclude that
if u say ur a wolf then u must be a wolf
u have to consider the flip side
what would a wolf do

imagine u were mafia
should u blatantly admit ur mafia?
of course not
because ur win condition depends on townies not knowing ur mafia
so admitting that ur mafia makes no sense if u want to win

so it makes no sense for either alignment
to blatantly tell everyone that they are mafia
therefore theres not much information that u can gain
if someone says "yes im mafia" during RQS
theyre most likely joking and it could come from either alignment

HOWEVER
the reason i pointed this post out earlier
was because of what dream said
she didnt outright go "im mafia" like i did in my RQS
she said something very particular
she said (paraphrased)
"i would joke and say im mafia but i dont want newbies to actually think im mafia"

now THAT is interesting
why
because first of all
like u said
most of these players have some experience
especially SE/ICs like mj and ND
if she had said "im mafia" then at the very least the SE/ICs would know that shes joking
and if newbies try to vote her out for it then we would explain it like i did above
so theres really not that much to fear from a misinterpreted joke

with that in mind
we see that dreams post suggests
she thought of the joke but consciously decided against making the joke
and that fits well with what i believe a newbie wolf player would do
wolves are generally more self-conscious than townies because of their burden of information
and we see that self-consciousness appear in this post

now of course
some town players
notably snow
are pretty self-conscious as town
and it very much depends on meta
so its not a concrete 100% scumread kind of thing
but generally speaking
without meta info on dream
i would say it has a very light scumlean

post_rendered:
normally i wouldnt respond to these types of attacks
but
as an SE who is supposed to be teaching newbies
i guess i will

ill paste the quotes here for convenience
awesomelion7 wrote:
he was asking around for who was a townlean
awesomelion7 wrote:
cuz the mafia want to know who's a townlean so that they can kill them

first of all
that first post is a misrepresentation
i was asking for reads in general
could be townreads could be scumreads
idc
the purpose was to stimulate discussion
bc in case u havent noticed
this game is really really inactive
weve just barely broken 100 posts and its been a week into day 1

ignoring that misrep
lets look at the actual argument

here are two reasons
why asking for townreads does not imply mafia

first reason
mafia benefits more from everyone not knowing each others townreads
bc the more information that townies post in the thread
the more coordination they have
the more accurate their reads and hangs are
and the worse the gamestate is for the mafia
wolves would love it if nobody posted any reads at all
now the counterpoint is that obviously as an SE
i do feel like i have an obligation to make this newbie game educational
so even if i was wolf
i probably would have tried to stir up some sort of discussion
but just so u know
in any normal game
its very rare to see wolves actively stirring up discussion and asking for reads
when the entire thread is completely dead
and everyone is completely apathetic

second reason
one of the best strategies to find mafia as a townie
is thru process of elimination (PoE)
which is basically a fancy way of saying
lets find a bunch of people that we are 100% sure are town (called the towncore)
then start hanging everyone else (called the PoE)
this is a very accurate method of finding scum
bc townreads are a lot more accurate than wolfreads
so a player asking for townreads is likely just trying to establish a towncore

so yea
asking for townreads doesnt necessarily imply mafia
in fact
most of the time its towny
but it depends a lot on context just like most tells in the game

if (c.getMonth() < 10) {

let hour = c.getHours();

let weekdays = ["Sunday", "Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"];
let months = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"];

function dateDiffInDays(a, b) {
    const _MS_PER_DAY = 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24;
    // Discard the time and time-zone information.
    const utc1 = Date.UTC(a.getFullYear(), a.getMonth(), a.getDate());
    const utc2 = Date.UTC(b.getFullYear(), b.getMonth(), b.getDate());

    return Math.floor((utc2 - utc1) / _MS_PER_DAY);
}

    } else if (weekdays.some((v) => latestPostTime[i].includes(v))) {
        if (latestPostTime[i].includes("PM")) {
        latestPostTime[i] =
            hour + 48 - getTime(i);
        } else if (latestPostTime[i].includes("AM")) {
            latestPostTime[i] =
                hour +
                48 -
                getTime(i) + 12;
        }
    } else {
        if (months.indexOf(latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[0]) + 1 < 10) {
            tempArr[2] = "0" + (months.indexOf(latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[0]) + 1);
        } else {
            tempArr[2] = months.indexOf(latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[0]) + 1;
        }
        if (latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[1].split(",")[0] < 10) {
            tempArr[3] = "0" + latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[1].split(",")[0];
        } else {
            tempArr[3] = latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[1].split(",")[0];
        }
        if (latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[3].split(":")[0] < 10) {
            tempArr[4] = "0" + latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[3].split(":")[0]
        } else {
            tempArr[4] = latestPostTime[i].split(" ")[3].split(":")[0]
            if (tempArr[4] == 12) {
                tempArr[4] = 0
            }
        }
        if (latestPostTime[i].includes("PM")) {
            tempArr[4] = parseInt(tempArr[4])+ 12
        }
        postTime = new Date(
        `${latestPostTime[i].split(", ")[1]}-${tempArr[2]}-${tempArr[3]}T${tempArr[4]}:00:00`
        );
        latestPostTime[i] = (d - postTime) / (1000 * 60 * 60) + 24; //ok like for some reason it's off by 24 hours so i just did this
        //a message to my future self
        // i hate myself
    }
} //`${userList[i]} (${latestPostTime[i]} hour/s ago)`
var activeList = "";
var unactiveList = "";
var pmList = "";
var replaceList = "";
var threadLink = ""
if (document.querySelector(".cmty-topic-subject").lastChild.nodeName === "A") {
    threadLink = document.querySelector(".cmty-topic-subject").lastChild.getAttribute("href");
} else {
    if (window.location.pathname.includes("s1")) {
        threadLink = window.location.pathname.split("s1")[0]
    } else {
        if (window.location.pathname.includes("_")) {
            threadLink = window.location.pathname.split("_")[0];
        } else {
            threadLink = window.location.pathname;
        }
    }
}

console.log(document.querySelector(".cmty-topic-subject").lastChild)
function linkGenerator(i) {
return (
    "[url=https://artofproblemsolving.com" +
    threadLink +
    "n" +
    latestPost[i] +
    "]#" +
    latestPost[i] +
    "[/url]"
);
}
var usernameEndText = "`[*]${userList[i]} (${latestPostTime[i]} hour/s ago ${linkGenerator(i)})\n`"
for (let i = 0; i < latestPostTime.length; i++) {
    if (latestPostTime[i] < unactiveLim) {
        activeList = activeList + eval(usernameEndText)
    } else if (latestPostTime[i] < pmProdLim) {
        unactiveList = unactiveList + eval(usernameEndText)
    } else if (latestPostTime[i] < replaceLim) {
        pmList = pmList + eval(usernameEndText)
    } else {
        replaceList = replaceList + eval(usernameEndText)
    }
}
function addUsers(i) {
    return `[*]${userList[i]} (${latestPostTime[i]} hour/s ago ${linkGenerator(i)})\n`;
}

postLines[0] = `[color=${postColor}][b]Activity check X.x[/b]\n`;
postLines[1] = `[color=${postColor}][b]Active:[/b]\n[list=disc]${activeList}[/list]\n`;
postLines[2] = `[color=${postColor}][b]Unactive:[/b]\n[list=disc]${unactiveList}[/list]\n`;
postLines[3] = `[color=${postColor}][b]PM Prod:[/b]\n[list=disc]${pmList}[/list]\n`;
postLines[4] = `[color=${postColor}][b]Replacement:[/b]\n[list=disc]${replaceList}[/list]`;
var postText = "";
for (let i = 0; i < postLines.length; i++) {
    postText = postText + postLines[i];
}
console.log(postText);
if (document.querySelector(".cmty-post-textarea")) {
    addContent()
} else {
    document.querySelector(".cmty-topic-mini-reply").click()
    addContent()
}
function addContent() {
    document.querySelector(".cmty-post-textarea").value = postText;
}

post_rendered:
Hello everyone,

It occurred to me that this never actually gets explained, so let me give you the really, really basic rundown of how this game works.

Everyone here is either town, who wants to kill the mafia by voting them out, or mafia, who want to live long enough to kill the entire town. Townies don’t want to get voted out, because that’s more time that the mafia isn’t getting voted out, and the mafia doesn’t want to get voted out for obvious reasons. Town has the majority of votes, so the voting will be, for the most part, meant to kill the mafia.

This means that everyone wants others to think that they are town.

This means you shouldn’t take it too seriously when someone says “I’m town” or “I’m scum” (scum meaning mafia). If they say “I’m town”, well duh, anyone would say that, and if they say “I’m scum”, they’re clearly joking.

Now, for voting people out, or hanging.

Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting. Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. Most scumhunting is just intuition. Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. We (me, Socoobo, mathjay, NDMath) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, do not claim your role for no reason. If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

Oh btw I’m town. Oh btw I’m scum.

Let the games begin.

post_rendered:
NDMath wrote:
Power Roles
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get killed the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia killed is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we hang tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.
